mn16:1.2: At one time the *BhagavƒÅ* was dwelling at SƒÅvatthƒ´, in Jeta‚Äôs Grove, AnƒÅthapi·πá·∏çika‚Äôs park.
mn16:10.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is not free of passion for *r≈´pa* ‚Ä¶
mn16:10.2: in this way this third bondage of the mind has not been cut off in him.
mn16:11.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu*, having eaten as much as he wants, filling his belly, dwells devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of stretching out, the pleasure of sleep.
mn16:11.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who, having eaten as much as he wants, filling his belly, dwells devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of stretching out, the pleasure of sleep ‚Äî his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:11.3: When his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fourth bondage of the mind has not been cut off in him.
mn16:12.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* lives the holy life aspiring to some class of devas: ‚ÄòBy this virtue, or observance, or austerity, or holy life, I will become a deva or one among the devas.‚Äô
mn16:12.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who lives the holy life aspiring to some class of devas: ‚ÄòBy this virtue, or observance, or austerity, or holy life, I will become a deva or one among the devas‚Äô ‚Äî his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:12.3: When his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fifth bondage of the mind has not been cut off in him.
mn16:12.4: These are the five bondages of the mind that have not been cut off in him.
mn16:13.1: Any *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, in whom these five mental barrennesses have not been abandoned and these five bondages of the mind have not been cut off ‚Äî that he would attain growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*]: no such possibility exists.
mn16:14.1: Any *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, in whom the five mental barrennesses have been abandoned and the five bondages of the mind have been thoroughly cut off ‚Äî that he would attain growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*]: this is possible.
mn16:15.1: And what five mental barrennesses have been abandoned in him?
mn16:15.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* does not doubt the Teacher, is not uncertain, is convinced, becomes confident.
mn16:15.3: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who does not doubt the Teacher, is not uncertain, is convinced, becomes confident ‚Äî his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:15.4: When his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this first mental barrenness has been abandoned in him.
mn16:16.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* does not doubt the *dhamma*, is not uncertain, is convinced, becomes confident ‚Ä¶
mn16:16.2: in this way this second mental barrenness has been abandoned in him.
mn16:17.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* does not doubt the *sa·πÖgha*, is not uncertain, is convinced, becomes confident ‚Ä¶
mn16:17.2: in this way this third mental barrenness has been abandoned in him.
mn16:18.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* does not doubt the training, is not uncertain, is convinced, becomes confident ‚Ä¶
mn16:18.2: in this way this fourth mental barrenness has been abandoned in him.
mn16:19.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is not angry with his companions in the holy life, not displeased, with an unwounded mind, not become barren.
mn16:19.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is not angry with his companions in the holy life, not displeased, with an unwounded mind, not become barren ‚Äî his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:19.3: When his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fifth mental barrenness has been abandoned in him.
mn16:19.4: These are the five mental barrennesses that have been abandoned in him.
mn16:2.1: ‚ÄúAny *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, in whom the five mental barrennesses have not been abandoned and the five bondages of the mind have not been cut off ‚Äî that he would attain growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*]: no such possibility exists.
mn16:20.1: And what five bondages of the mind have been thoroughly cut off in him?
mn16:20.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is free of passion for sensual pleasures, with desire gone, with affection gone, with thirst gone, with fever gone, with *ta·πáhƒÅ* gone.
mn16:20.3: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is free of passion for sensual pleasures, with desire gone, with affection gone, with thirst gone, with fever gone, with *ta·πáhƒÅ* gone ‚Äî his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:20.4: When his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this first bondage of the mind has been thoroughly cut off in him.
mn16:21.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is free of passion for the body ‚Ä¶
mn16:22.1: He is free of passion for *r≈´pa* ‚Ä¶
mn16:23.1: He does not, having eaten as much as he wants, filling his belly, dwell devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of stretching out, the pleasure of sleep.
mn16:23.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who does not, having eaten as much as he wants, filling his belly, dwell devoted to the pleasure of lying down, the pleasure of stretching out, the pleasure of sleep ‚Äî his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:23.3: When his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fourth bondage of the mind has been thoroughly cut off in him.
mn16:24.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* does not live the holy life aspiring to some class of devas: ‚ÄòBy this virtue, or observance, or austerity, or holy life, I will become a deva or one among the devas.‚Äô
mn16:24.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who does not live the holy life aspiring to some class of devas: ‚ÄòBy this virtue, or observance, or austerity, or holy life, I will become a deva or one among the devas‚Äô ‚Äî his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:24.3: When his mind inclines to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fifth bondage of the mind has been thoroughly cut off in him.
mn16:24.4: These are the five bondages of the mind that have been thoroughly cut off in him.
mn16:25.1: Any *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, in whom these five mental barrennesses have been abandoned and these five bondages of the mind have been thoroughly cut off ‚Äî that he would attain growth, increase, and expansion in this *dhamma* and *vinaya* [*dhammavinaye*]: this is possible.
mn16:26.1: He develops the basis for power endowed with *samƒÅdhi* through desire and accompanied by striving-*sa·πÖkhƒÅras* [*chandasamƒÅdhipadhƒÅnasa·πÖkhƒÅrasamannƒÅgata·πÅ*],
mn16:26.2: he develops the basis for power endowed with *samƒÅdhi* through energy and accompanied by striving-*sa·πÖkhƒÅras* [*vƒ´riyasamƒÅdhipadhƒÅnasa·πÖkhƒÅrasamannƒÅgata·πÅ*],
mn16:26.3: he develops the basis for power endowed with *samƒÅdhi* through mind [*citta*] and accompanied by striving-*sa·πÖkhƒÅras* [*cittasamƒÅdhipadhƒÅnasa·πÖkhƒÅrasamannƒÅgata·πÅ*],
mn16:26.4: he develops the basis for power endowed with *samƒÅdhi* through inquiry and accompanied by striving-*sa·πÖkhƒÅras* [*vƒ´ma·πÅsƒÅsamƒÅdhipadhƒÅnasa·πÖkhƒÅrasamannƒÅgata·πÅ*]; intense effort is the fifth.
mn16:27.1: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, thus endowed with these fifteen factors together with intense effort, is capable of disenchantment, capable of full awakening, capable of attaining the unsurpassed security from bondage.
mn16:27.10: Mental Barrenness, the sixth, is finished.
mn16:27.2: Just as, *bhikkhus*, a hen might have eight or ten or twelve eggs.
mn16:27.3: And those eggs had been properly sat on by that hen, properly warmed, properly incubated.
mn16:27.4: Even if that hen should not have such a wish:
mn16:27.5: ‚ÄòOh, if only these chicks might safely break out, splitting the shell with the tip of their claws or with their beaks!‚Äô
mn16:27.6: Still, those chicks are capable of safely breaking out, splitting the shell with the tip of their claws or with their beaks.
mn16:27.7: So too, *bhikkhus*, that *bhikkhu*, thus endowed with these fifteen factors together with intense effort, is capable of disenchantment, capable of full awakening, capable of attaining the unsurpassed security from bondage.‚Äù
mn16:27.9: Satisfied, those *bhikkhus* delighted in the *BhagavƒÅ*‚Äôs words.
mn16:3.1: And what five mental barrennesses have not been abandoned in him?
mn16:3.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* doubts the Teacher, is uncertain, is not convinced, does not become confident.
mn16:3.3: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who doubts the Teacher, is uncertain, is not convinced, does not become confident ‚Äî his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:3.4: When his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this first mental barrenness has not been abandoned in him.
mn16:4.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* doubts the *dhamma*, is uncertain, is not convinced, does not become confident ‚Ä¶
mn16:4.2: in this way this second mental barrenness has not been abandoned in him.
mn16:5.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* doubts the *sa·πÖgha*, is uncertain, is not convinced, does not become confident ‚Ä¶
mn16:5.2: in this way this third mental barrenness has not been abandoned in him.
mn16:6.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* doubts the training, is uncertain, is not convinced, does not become confident.
mn16:6.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who doubts the training, is uncertain, is not convinced, does not become confident ‚Äî his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:6.3: When his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fourth mental barrenness has not been abandoned in him.
mn16:7.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is angry with his companions in the holy life, displeased, with a wounded mind, become barren.
mn16:7.2: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is angry with his companions in the holy life, displeased, with a wounded mind, become barren ‚Äî his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:7.3: When his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this fifth mental barrenness has not been abandoned in him.
mn16:7.4: These are the five mental barrennesses that have not been abandoned in him.
mn16:8.1: And what five bondages of the mind have not been cut off in him?
mn16:8.2: Here, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is not free of passion for sensual pleasures, with desire not gone, with affection not gone, with thirst not gone, with fever not gone, with *ta·πáhƒÅ* not gone.
mn16:8.3: That *bhikkhu*, *bhikkhus*, who is not free of passion for sensual pleasures, with desire not gone, with affection not gone, with thirst not gone, with fever not gone, with *ta·πáhƒÅ* not gone ‚Äî his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving.
mn16:8.4: When his mind does not incline to ardency, application, continuity, and striving, in this way this first bondage of the mind has not been cut off in him.
mn16:9.1: Furthermore, *bhikkhus*, a *bhikkhu* is not free of passion for the body ‚Ä¶
mn16:9.2: in this way this second bondage of the mind has not been cut off in him.
